title: Weekly Insights Review
summary:
Read here everything you need to know


Latest Trends
The European Commission has given its approval for a €1.1 billion scheme to support the roll-out of carbon capture and storage (CCS) technologies in Denmark. Read More

Market has some depth
Following a quite uninspiring second half of 2022, Baltic Dry Index stepped into the New Year with a rather tepid and lukewarm feeling. Read More

Markets have embraced a more positive narrative
Despite the Fed's persistently hawkish rhetoric, since the last quarter of 2022, markets have embraced a more positive narrative, mostly on the back of increasingly benign US inflation numbers.

children.title: Snapshot of Spot Freight Rates, Supply-Demand Trends, Port Congestions
section_path: Snapshot of Spot Freight Rates, Supply-Demand Trends, Port Congestions
children.section_path_text: Snapshot of Spot Freight Rates, Supply-Demand Trends, Port Congestions
children.summary: The start of the new year brings weaker sentiment in freight rates, with an increase in the number of ballast vessels and significant downward revisions in demand growth.

children.title: China continues to experience industrial weakness and of course great problems in its housing market
section_path: China continues to experience industrial weakness and of course great problems in its housing market
children.section_path_text: China continues to experience industrial weakness and of course great problems in its housing market
children.summary: One key theme we have been stressing in recent work is that passenger travel in China via road, subways, and air are increasing (along with other consumer activity), but some industrial markets in China continue to face significant pressure.

children.title: Macro/Geopolitics, Commodity Markets, Freight and Bunker Markets
section_path: Macro/Geopolitics, Commodity Markets, Freight and Bunker Markets
children.section_path_text: Macro/Geopolitics, Commodity Markets, Freight and Bunker Markets
children.summary: Chinese growth during last year's final quarter proved more resilient than most economists had expected.

children.title: Higher lows/higher highs could be on the cards this year
section_path: Higher lows/higher highs could be on the cards this year
children.section_path_text: Higher lows/higher highs could be on the cards this year
children.summary: As dry bulk activity resumes following the Christmas holidays, traders are faced with the anticipated seasonal slowdown and thus falling spot freight rates across all asset classes.

children.title: It began the year contracting on a year-on-year basis in both January and February
section_path: It began the year contracting on a year-on-year basis in both January and February
children.section_path_text: It began the year contracting on a year-on-year basis in both January and February
children.summary: India produced approximately 115.7 billion kilowatt hours of electricity last month, which is up month-on-month by 7.2 billion kilowatt hours (7%) and up year-on-year by 9.4 billion kilowatt hours (9%).

children.title: Sentiment was broadly positive as optimism regarding China's reopening returned
section_path: Sentiment was broadly positive as optimism regarding China's reopening returned
children.section_path_text: Sentiment was broadly positive as optimism regarding China's reopening returned
children.summary: A slightly weaker USD also added support.

children.title: INDIA SURPASSES CHINA AS LARGEST IMPORTER OF SEABORNE RUSSIAN CRUDE
section_path: INDIA SURPASSES CHINA AS LARGEST IMPORTER OF SEABORNE RUSSIAN CRUDE
children.section_path_text: INDIA SURPASSES CHINA AS LARGEST IMPORTER OF SEABORNE RUSSIAN CRUDE
children.summary: Russia exported record-high volumes of crude to India in 1H January, facilitated by tonnage supply from existing and incumbent shipowners.

children.title: Happy Bunny?
children.summary: Chinese New Year, also known as Lunar New Year or Spring Festival, is just around the corner; January 22nd, 2023 marks the beginning of the Chinese Year of the Rabbit and celebrations will wrap up with Lantern Festival on February 5th, 2023.
